result:
**REST:** Simple mental model, mature tooling, natural caching. Server defines shape of response. Client gets all-or-nothing. Good for straightforward CRUD; bad when frontend needs varied subsets of data (mobile vs web vs desktop each want different fields).

**GraphQL:** Client specifies exact fields needed, single request replaces multiple REST calls. No over/under-fetching. Steeper learning curve, caching harder (HTTP caching doesn't work as well), requires query parsing/validation server-side, debugging trickier. Query complexity can explode if you're not careful about rate limiting.

**Pick REST if:** Data shapes are stable, caching matters, team speed matters more than flexibility, you don't have wildly different client needs.

**Pick GraphQL if:** Clients have radically different data requirements (mobile, web, third-party), over-fetching is a real bandwidth problem, or you're building an API platform others will consume and you want to minimize versioning.

**Hybrid** often wins: REST for stable high-volume endpoints, GraphQL for exploratory/flexible use cases.
